name: Ben Carson
subject: An important thing to remember this week...
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Carson America, Inc
body:
---
| | [](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/32441638:WP32U7yrN:m:1:876315805:B958F4ACB5B1C70F718D3F9DE7F9C71E:r)
---

Peter,

It's almost Christmas, and I know that many of us spend this week frantically rushing around buying presents, our time occupied with "how much to spend" on loved ones rather than spending much time occupied _with_ those loved ones.

It's about relationships, not retail.

We should remind ourselves of that more often -- not just as it relates to our immediate families but also our nation as a whole.

In many ways, we are one big American family.

_E pluribus unum_. Out of many, one. Black or white, rich or poor, we are all endowed by our Creator with certain unalienable rights. We are all Americans, and we're all in this together.

I've spent my life as a healer, and that's a quality I hope to bring to the White House. Sadly, our political class has succeeded in driving wedges between too many of our citizens. We are supposed to think that we are each other's enemies, but it is just not true.
The only way that Washington can subjugate "We the People" is if we are divided. For America's survival, we just can't allow that to happen.

So I thank you for joining me in this monumental endeavor.

[**As we get closer and closer to people casting their votes, I hope you will continue to support my campaign. The truth is I cannot do this without your help.**](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/32441638:WP32U7yrN:m:1:876315805:B958F4ACB5B1C70F718D3F9DE7F9C71E:r)

So over this week, I hope you will treasure your time with your family, friends and loved ones.

In particular, we should all look into the faces of our children and grandchildren. They are the future not just of our family, but of our country.

We should strive to do everything in our power to set them up for success.

Thank you for your time, your prayers, and [your continued support](http://email.bencarson.com/ct/32441638:WP32U7yrN:m:1:876315805:B958F4ACB5B1C70F718D3F9DE7F9C71E:r).

Sincerely,

Ben Carson

body:
| | | | | | | | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/45512184:JNwRiNrmN:m:1:1349778030:55654CA5C6B69884C90885B8E74AB6F8:r)
---
| Dear Peter, Right now I'm traveling through New Hampshire with my family, meeting as many voters as I can, before we pause to celebrate Christmas. It hasn't been always easy getting the Rubios together for a quality Christmas picture -- [**see for yourself.**](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/45512185:JNwRiNrmN:m:1:1349778030:55654CA5C6B69884C90885B8E74AB6F8:r)
But I think this year's effort did turn out okay:
---
| | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/45512185:JNwRiNrmN:m:1:1349778030:55654CA5C6B69884C90885B8E74AB6F8:r)
---
| This time of year isn't just about family -- it's about celebrating the greatest gift humanity ever received: the birth of our Savior, Jesus Christ. I often say that the most important job I'll ever have is to be a father, and the most important part of that, really, is to teach my kids about the gifts of our faith. Throughout the season of Advent, I've been getting ready with my family for this special holiday, a celebration of an event that changed the world for eternity. I hope that you've been able to do that, too: to reflect on whatever faith you hold, and share it with those you love. My family has been extraordinarily blessed this year, and I hope yours has, too. This Christmas, when there's so much turmoil, violence, and suffering in the world, I pray that every one of us can find some peace with family and faith. Merry Christmas, and best wishes for a blessed New Year,
Marco Rubio

name: Rand Paul
subject: End the reckless spending
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Patriot,
Like most conservatives, I believe the
country is headed in the wrong direction.
Unlike my Republican colleagues, I'm willing
recognize that the greatest threat to our nation is a debt crisis
caused by cowardly politicians unwilling to be fiscally
responsible.
Although we currently spend roughly $7
million per minute and face a debt of over $18 trillion, our
Republican-controlled Congress recently passed a new spending
bill that will add $700 billion in additional debt.
This reckless habit of spending money we
don't have has gone on for far too long, and we must face fiscal
reality immediately.
It's time to stop spending more than what
comes in.

If you agree please
commit to a generous contribution TODAY!

This week, I issued a special Christmas
edition of "The Waste Report" to highlight some of the most
egregious examples of reckless government spending. Since I began
publishing the Waste Report, I've discovered that we have spent
taxpayer money on everything from:

$6 million campaign to promote tourism in
Albania

$43 million gas station in Afghanistan

$15 million grant to allow a golf club
maker to do research in space

$50,000 Christmas tree television ads

$15.6 million grant to cover foreign
students' college tuition

$175 million study on sleep apnea riddled
with fraud

$450,000 grant to produce climate change
video games

$25,000 laundromat for the U.S. Forest
Service

$100,00 flower show for the National Park
Service

$380,000 study to investigate the
"Freshman 15″

$218,000 campaign to sterilize feral
coyotes and invasive burros

$15,000 grant for a conference on balding

That's right-despite our out-of-control debt
resulting in fewer Americans working than at any other time since
the Jimmy Carter administration, we're still promoting Christmas
trees and building an unusable gas station in a war zone.
There is clearly hundreds of millions of
dollars worth of pork barrel spending that can and should be
removed from our list of expenditures. Yet, my Republican
colleagues-including fellow presidential candidates Sens. Ted
Cruz and Marco Rubio-refuse to cut even a penny.
This March, Cruz and Rubio wanted to increase
military spending by $190 million over the next two years. I
called for raising defense spending by the same amount, but also
proposed offsetting the hike with proportional cuts in other
areas of the budget.
Cruz, Rubio, and nearly every other
Republican in the Senate voted against my amendment.
Fiscal responsibility is apparently much
easier to say than to do.
The problem in Washington is that there is an
unholy alliance between right and left that comes together to
spend more of your money at every turn.
Conservatives want more military spending.
Liberals want more domestic spending. As a result, they shake
hands and agree to spend more on everything.
Congress will never balance the budget unless
we force them to do so. That's why I have advocated for a
balanced budget admendment to the Constitution for years.
We cannot trust careless, fiscally
irresponsible bureaucrats to slow spending.
Although they all ran on fiscally
conservative platforms, our last three Republican presidents
added over $9 trillion to our national debt.
Today, the economy is simply too frail to
weather more broken promises from conservatives running to lead
the nation into the future.
As 2016 approaches us, I encourage my
colleagues and opponents to begin thinking long and hard about
the kind of future they want their children and grandchildren to
have, as well as what they will change in the new year in order
to make those expectations possible.
Will you stand with me?

In Liberty,
Rand Paul

Pitch in $25 TODAY >>

Pitch in $50 TODAY >>

Pitch in $75 TODAY >>

Pitch in $100 TODAY>>

Or donate another
amount >>

Paid for by Rand Paul for President

name: Katie McGinty
subject: ADD YOUR NAME to support Paid Family Leave
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate
body:
---
Friend --

It's a choice no parent should ever have to make: the choice between caring for a loved one and keeping a job.

And yet millions of Americans do, every day, because **the United States is the only industrialized nation in the world that does not mandate paid family leave.**

In these tough economic times, too many workers are already struggling to balance the demands of work and family. That's why we should be doing everything we can to make it easier for them to take time off to care for a family member or a newborn without fear of losing their paychecks or even their jobs.

**[Join me in telling Congress that in 2016, Americans deserve paid family leave.](https://act.myngp.com/el/8283939513852493824/-5915738694806929408?ms=20151223&refcode=e20151223&refcode2=e20151223&amount=15)**

| [](https://act.myngp.com/el/8283939513852493824/-5843681100769001472?ms=20151223&refcode=e20151223&refcode2=e20151223&amount=15)
---

**A recent study found that almost a quarter of new mothers are back at work within two weeks of giving birth because they can 't afford to forgo their paycheck.** And while every family may experience unexpected illness, only 13% of workers have any access to the paid leave necessary to look after a sick family member or a newborn child.

Circumstances that warrant paid leave are difficult enough as it is. Risking a paycheck or a job should not be an added burden to hardworking Americans who want to both provide financially for their families and care for them when they're ill.

**[Add your name here to support paid family leave for _all_ workers in the coming new year.](https://act.myngp.com/el/8283939513852493824/-5915738694806929408?ms=20151223&refcode=e20151223&refcode2=e20151223&amount=15)**

The truth of the matter is that paid family leave is not just a social benefit to our society -- it's an economic one as well. The private sector has already discovered the advantages of allowing their employees time to care for their families -- Companies like Netflix, Microsoft, Amazon, and Spotify have all recently announced leave policies that don't leave workers worried about loved ones and anxious about their paychecks at the same time. Now, it's time the rest of our country did, too.

81% of the public agrees that it's time for the United States to step up and take its place among the other advanced nations of the world that support families and businesses both. Let's make it happen in 2016 -- before another year goes by without the support American workers both need and deserve.

Katie
| [**DONATE**](https://act.myngp.com/el/8283939513852493824/-5771623506731073536?refcode=e20151223&refcode2=e20151223&amount=15)

name: Doug Stafford
subject: Eight days
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for President
body:
Dear Patriot, It is really starting to come down to the wire. **There are only 40 days until the first votes are cast, and only 22 days until the next presidential debate in South Carolina.** Rand is in this race to win it, and he is counting on your help every step of the way. The next important date we face is the FEC's Fourth Quarter fundraising deadline, which ends December 31 at midnight. As the ball drops on a new year, it also drops on this very important deadline. Let me tell you why these next eight days are so vital. **As you may have seen, Fox announced new criteria for the next debate and is attempting to shrink the number of people on the main stage again**. This time, the top six nationally or top five in Iowa and New Hampshire make it in. **Media reports will always be wrong, so let me be clear.** Under this criteria, Rand would have made the last debate and been on the main stage. Despite this, I have no doubt that the media and the establishment will continue to try to silence our voice leading up to the next debate. The last several debates they unfairly predicted Rand would not be on stage. Yet even with their continued negativity directed at us, Rand has been on the main stage every time. With your help, Rand will defy the media and the Big Government establishment again, and -- with your help -- claim his rightful place on the main debate stage. But to ensure this campaign heads into January with a head of steam, [we have set a goal of $500,000 by the end of this quarter.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32460106:WOohlcyKN:m:1:879035888:0B1318445754B8A7C7320CD12EE093D4:r) | | [Please commit to a generous contribution TODAY!](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32460107:WOohlcyKN:m:1:879035888:0B1318445754B8A7C7320CD12EE093D4:r)
---
**The next debate is extremely important. And so is our ground game, voter contact and get-out-the-vote operations in the early states.**
We must fully fund those efforts, and [we can't do it without your help today](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32460106:WOohlcyKN:m:1:879035888:0B1318445754B8A7C7320CD12EE093D4:r).
So please join us by making an extra effort between now and December 31.
[Donate whatever you can. If you can, please stretch and be extra generous. Spread the word online. Sign up to volunteer. Urge your friends and family to chip in.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32460106:WOohlcyKN:m:1:879035888:0B1318445754B8A7C7320CD12EE093D4:r)
**Stand with Rand so he can stand on the main stage where he belongs.**
The last debate showed why he belongs there.
He took the fight to his opponents, drew a clear contrast by standing strong for the Constitution, and was seen as the winner in many post debate polls and analyses.
**So is it really any wonder why the national media continues to try and dismiss our message?**
With your help, we will move up as the voting nears, as we have always planned.
But we cannot do it without your help between now and December 31.
**I will be in touch more after Christmas.**
[But for now, get ready to Stand with Rand at this important time!](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32460106:WOohlcyKN:m:1:879035888:0B1318445754B8A7C7320CD12EE093D4:r)

In Liberty,
Doug Stafford
Chief Strategist

Last week, the Supreme Court ruled that corporations are able to fine consumers and not have to worry whether such fines _are even legal_.

Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg is passionately speaking out against this monumental win for corporations at the expense of everyday Americans.

**[Join her and sign your name to stop corporations from trampling on consumer rights!](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea761/e60883a/7028d160/14420aa8/3448705584/VEsE/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/stand-with-RBG?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.22_B1_scotus_X__F1_S1_C1__all")** | ****[SIGN YOUR NAME->](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea761/e60883a/7028d160/14420aa8/3448705584/VEsF/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/stand-with-RBG?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.22_B1_scotus_X__F1_S1_C1__all")****
---
Friends --

**The Supreme Court just gave corporations and Big Business ANOTHER huge handout.**

Last week, in the case _DIRECTV v. Imburgia_ , the Supreme Court ruled that corporations can effectively immunize themselves from many lawsuits involving illegal fees.

That means Big Business can "squeeze money out of your consumers without having to worry about whether doing so is illegal."[1]

According to the _New York Times_ , this is "the latest in a series of Supreme Court decisions that have made it harder for consumers to go to court to pursue claims of fraud and defective products."[2]

_**Luckily, Ruth Bader Ginsburg is fighting back!**_

In a scathing dissent, Justice Ginsburg defended the rights of the people -- and called out big corporations for their insatiable greed.

**Stand With Ruth Bader Ginsburg:[Don't let corporations strip consumer rights >>](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea761/e60883a/7028d160/14420aa8/3448705584/VEsC/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/stand-with-RBG?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.22_B1_scotus_X__F1_S1_C1__all")**

---
We're grateful that Justice Ginsburg continues to stand up for the rights of the people -- and against corporations trying to strip Americans of their consumer rights.

**So join Justice Ginsburg and please sign your name right now:**

[**http://go.boldpac.com/stand-with-RBG**](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea761/e60883a/7028d160/14420aa8/3448705584/VEsA/ "http://go.boldpac.com/page/s/stand-with-RBG?source=MS_EM_PET_2015.12.22_B1_scotus_X__F1_S1_C1__all")

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: Single-payer health care
body:
#
**[I want to make health care a right for every American. The health care industry doesn't like that very much, so they're flooding my opponents with cash. Fight back against those who want to stop a Medicare-for-all, single-payer system with a $3 contribution to our campaign.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb2814/43f17890/4bbc17e7/6213a382/903974720/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/single-payer?refcode=em151223-fight-back-against-the-attacks-full")**
* * *
Sisters and Brothers -
I want to talk with you about one of the very real differences between Secretary Clinton and me that surfaced during last weekend's debate, and that is our approach to health care in this country.
**I was, and all progressives should be, deeply disappointed in some of her attacks on a Medicare-for-all, single-payer health care system.** The health insurance lobbyists and big pharmaceutical companies try to make "national health care" sound scary. It is not.
In fact, a large single-payer system already exists in the United States. It's called Medicare and the people enrolled give it high marks. More importantly, it has succeeded in providing near-universal coverage to Americans over age 65 in a very cost-effective manner.
So I want to go over some facts with you and ask that you take action on this important issue:
Right now, because of the gains made under the Affordable Care Act, 17 million people have health care who did not before the law was passed. This is a good start, and something we should be proud of. **But we can do better.**
The truth is, it is a national disgrace that the United States is the only major country that does not guarantee health care to all people as a right. Today, 29 million of our sisters and brothers are without care. Not only are deductibles rising, but the cost of prescription drugs is skyrocketing as well. There is a major crisis in primary health care in the United States.
So I start my approach to health care from two very simple premises:
1\. Health care must be recognized as a right, not a privilege -- every man, woman and child in our country should be able to access quality care regardless of their income.
2\. We must create a national system to provide care for every single American in the most cost-effective way possible.
I expected to take some heat on these fundamental beliefs during a general election, but since it is already happening in the Democratic primary, I want to address some of the critiques made by Secretary Clinton and Rupert Murdoch's _Wall Street Journal_ directly:
Under my plan, we will lower the cost of health care for the average family making $50,000 a year by nearly $5,000 a year. It is unfair to say simply how much more a program will cost without letting people know we are doing away with the cost of private insurance and that the middle class will be paying substantially less for health care under a single-payer system than Hillary Clinton's program. Attacking the cost of the plan without acknowledging the bottom-line savings is the way Republicans have attacked this idea for decades. Taking that approach in a Democratic Primary undermines the hard work of so many who have fought to guarantee health care as a right in this country, and it hurts our prospects for achieving that goal in the near future. I hope that it stops.
Let me also be clear that a Medicare-for-all, single-payer health care system will expand employment by lifting a major financial weight off of the businesses burdened by employee health expenses. And for the millions of Americans who are currently in jobs they don't like but must stay put because of health care access, they would be free to explore more productive opportunities as they desire.
So, what is stopping us from guaranteeing free, quality health care as a basic fundamental right for all Americans? I believe the answer ties into campaign finance reform.
**The truth is, the insurance companies and the drug companies are bribing the United States Congress.**
**[I want to make health care a right for every American. The health care industry doesn't like that very much, so they're flooding my opponents with cash. Fight back against those who want to stop a Medicare-for-all, single-payer system with a $3 contribution to our campaign.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb2814/43f17890/4bbc17e7/6213a382/903974720/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/single-payer?refcode=em151223-fight-back-against-the-attacks-full")**
Now, I don't go around asking millionaires and billionaires for money. You know that. I don't think I'm going to get a whole lot of contributions from the health care and pharmaceutical industries. I don't like to kick a man when he is down, but when some bad actors have tried to contribute to our campaign, like the pharmaceutical CEO Martin Shkreli who jacked up the price of a life saving drug for AIDS patients, I donated his contribution to an AIDS clinic in Washington, D.C.
Secretary Clinton, on the other hand, has received millions of dollars from the health care and pharmaceutical industries, a number that is sure to rise as time goes on. Since 1998, there are no industries that have spent more money to influence legislators than these two. Billions of dollars! An absolutely obscene amount of money. And in this election cycle alone, Secretary Clinton has raised more money from the health care industry than did the top 3 Republicans -- combined.
Now, and let's not be naive about this, maybe they are dumb and don't know what they are going to get? But I don't think that's the case, and I don't believe you do either.
So, what can we do about it?
Changing the health care laws in this country in such a way that guarantees health care as a right and not a privilege will require nothing short of a political revolution. That's what this campaign is about and it is work we must continue long after I am elected the next President of the United States.
And because of the success we have enjoyed so far, I am more convinced today than ever before that universal quality health care as a right for all Americans will eventually become the law of the land.
It is the only way forward.
**[Make a $3 contribution to our campaign as a way of saying you have had ENOUGH of the health care and pharmaceutical lobby buying our candidates and elected officials.](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/m/24fb2814/43f17890/4bbc17e7/6213a382/903974720/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/single-payer?refcode=em151223-fight-back-against-the-attacks-full")**
Thank you for standing with me on this important issue.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

Friends, the Supreme Court agreed to hear YET ANOTHER lawsuit against the Affordable Care Act.

This time, right-wing groups are challenging a provision in the bill that requires employers to provide insurance plans that cover birth control and other basic forms of contraception.

**If the Supreme Court rules in their favor, it will be a _devastating_ setback not only to one of President Obama's greatest achievements, but for millions of American women as well.**

_We can 't let them get away with it._

[Add your name today to tell the Supreme Court to protect women's access to contraception >>](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea77b/e60883a/702eab85/14420b5c/4067255164/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/ "http://go.boldpac.com/Protect-Women?firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326")

As far as we're concerned, access to affordable contraceptive care is a fundamental right.

But the Supreme Court has a history of siding with anti-choice Republicans -- as evidenced by their ruling last year in the _Hobby Lobby_ case.

So if we're going to make sure they don't continue to chip away at women's rights and Obamacare, we'll need as many people as possible to sign this petition.

**Can we count on you to add your name?**

[**http://go.boldpac.com/Protect-Women**](http://go.boldpac.com/page/m/b7ea77b/e60883a/702eab85/14420b5c/4067255164/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0=/ "http://go.boldpac.com/Protect-Women?firstName=&lastName=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326")

With the government in general and Congress in particular. [That's why I'm running for President to restore fiscal sanity and destory the](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32464693:snrr-cyNT:m:1:879035888:B8C6D0FEDDEA7D34AB98291C31565FB5:r)[ Washington machine.](http://paracom.paramountcommunication.com/ct/32464694:snrr-cyNT:m:1:879035888:B8C6D0FEDDEA7D34AB98291C31565FB5:r) But today, I think it's important to express my grievances with my opponents in this race and the terrible spending policies that are sinking. name: Senator Patty Murray
subject: The difference women make
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate
body:
---
Friend --
I can't do it alone. Right wing Republicans are out in full force, doing absolutely everything they can to turn back the clock on women's health care, take away access to health care for families, and block progress on so many of the middle class priorities that I and other Democrats are fighting for. We've been able to stop the worst of these attacks so far, but this year's election will be absolutely _critical_ in determining if we can keep this up in 2017. The progress we've made for women and middle class families and everything we want to do to build on that progress is under threat. I am doing my best to fight back, but I can't do it alone. **That 's why I'm supporting Katie McGinty for U.S. Senate. I need Katie to join me in Washington so that together, we can fight back against these Republican attacks and make progress for middle class families.** [**Katie needs another 29 donors to step up before midnight tonight**** to stay on track with her campaign. Can you chip in $5 to help?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/625011994625310720/-7500442813687922688?refcode=e20151223.2&refcode2=e20151223.2nd&amount=5) I was the first female senator ever elected from Washington state, and I certainly understand the challenges women face in politics today. But as first female Chair of the Senate Veterans Affairs Committee, and the first female Chair of the Senate Budget Committee--I know the difference women can make when we get involved and fight to make a difference. **That 's exactly what Katie McGinty is doing now. Katie is running to become the first female senator from Pennsylvania, and I'm supporting her because I need her help in Washington.** The race in Pennsylvania could absolutely be _ the _race that determines control of the U.S. Senate. Losing this race hurts our chances to continue protecting women's health care, confirming pro-choice judges to the Supreme Court, and taking on important issues like paid sick leave and equal pay. **That 's why I need Katie and why Katie needs you. ** [**Click here to contribute $5 to Katie 's campaign, so that we can send a champion for women to Washington and put the Senate back in Democratic hands, where women's health care and rights will _always_ be protected.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/625011994625310720/-7500442813687922688?refcode=e20151223.2&refcode2=e20151223.2nd&amount=5) Thank you,
Senator Patty Murray

Right wing Republicans are out in full force, doing absolutely everything they can to turn back the clock on women's health care, take away access to health care for families, and block progress on so many of the middle class priorities that I and other Democrats are fighting for. We've been able to stop the worst of these attacks so far, but this year's election will be absolutely critical in determining if we can keep this up in 2017. The progress we've made for women and middle class families and everything we want to do to build on that progress is under threat. I am doing my best to fight back, but I can't do it alone. That 's why I'm supporting Katie McGinty for U.S. Senate. I need Katie to join me in Washington so that together, we can fight back against these Republican attacks and make progress for middle class families. I was the first female senator ever elected from Washington state, and I certainly understand the challenges women face in politics today. But as first female Chair of the Senate Veterans Affairs Committee, and the first female Chair of the Senate Budget Committee--I know the difference women can make when we get involved and fight to make a difference. That 's exactly what Katie McGinty is doing now. Katie is running to become the first female senator from Pennsylvania, and I'm supporting her because I need her help in Washington. The race in Pennsylvania could absolutely be the race that determines control of the U.S. Senate. Losing this race hurts our chances to continue protecting women's health care, confirming pro-choice judges to the Supreme Court, and taking on important issues like paid sick leave and equal pay. That 's why I need Katie and why Katie needs you. Thank you, Senator Patty Murray | | | Paid For By Katie McGinty for Senate
